description: This figure consists of two subplots showing thermodynamic prediction results for two low-density medium Mn steels, M1 (Fe-8Mn-3Al-0.2C, Si-free) and M2 (Fe-8Mn-3Al-1Si-0.2C, Si-containing). Subplot (a) illustrates the predicted equilibrium austenite fraction (%) as a function of intercritical annealing temperature (IAT, ranging from 600°C to 900°C): both steels show continuously increasing austenite fraction with rising IAT, and at the selected IAT of 700°C, M1 has ~47% austenite while M2 has ~32% austenite. Subplot (b) presents predicted elemental composition (wt.%) in austenite at different temperatures: austenite stabilizers Mn and C show decreasing concentration with rising IAT, while Al and Si concentrations change slightly, with M1 holding slightly higher Mn content than M2 at the same temperature. These results support the selection of 700°C as IAT, where Mn content in austenite reaches ~11 wt.% to ensure sufficient austenite stability against martensitic transformation during post-annealing cooling.
